title: Fecal output, score, and water content
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3dar6","text":"Protocol for measuring general signs of constipation in mouse models using number of fecal pellets animals are able to produce over a certain amount of time, Bristol stool scale scoring, and water content. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Habituate animals to behavior testing room for 1hr prior to start of assay.
Individually place each animal into empty, bedding-free, standard mouse cages.
Once 15 minutes are up, count the number of fecal pellets produced by each mouse in the cage.
Remove each animal for individual cages and transfer back into home cage.
Assess fecal pellets in empty cage and record a score based on the Bristol stool scale:

A score of 1 is given for fecal pellets that are small, dry, hard lumps, indicating severe constipation. A score of 2 is given for lumpy, dry, but sausage-like fecal pellets, indicating mild constipation. A score of 3 is given for mildly dry sausage-like pellets with cracks in the surface. A score of 4 is given for smooth, soft, sausage or snake-shaped pellets. Both scores of 3 and 4 are considered normal. A score of 5 is given for soft blobs with clear-cut edges. A score of 6 was given for fluffy pieces with ragged edges, indicating mild diarrhea. Lastly, a score of 7 is given for an entirely liquid consistency, indicating severe diarrhea (Lewis et al., 1997)

Collect 2-3 fecal pellets in pre-weighed tubes from each empty cage. and then pellets were incubated at 65℃ for 24 hours. Tubes and dry fecal pellets were re-weighed and percentage of water content calculated.
Weigh and record weight of tubes for fecal pellet collection (one per mouse).
Take the weight of the tube and wet fecal pellet and record.
Incubate pellets at 65℃ for 24 hours with tube caps open.
After incubation, re-weigh tubes and dry fecal pellets.
Calculate percentage of water content as the difference between wet and dry weight as a percentage of wet weight.
creator: Anastasiya Moiseyenko
